title: Daily Market Digest: Tech Sell-off Drags Indices Lower Amid Rate Hike Fears
subtitle: Friday, June 05, 2026 - Market Close

items.headline: US Markets Experience Sharp Decline as Tech Stocks Plummet
items.summary: The U.S. stock market recorded its worst day since October on Friday, June 5, 2026, primarily driven by a significant sell-off in high-flying technology and AI stocks. This downturn was exacerbated by a stronger-than-expected May jobs report, which fueled expectations of potential Federal Reserve interest rate hikes later in the year, and a surge in Treasury yields. Overall market sentiment is decidedly bearish, with Wall Street's VIX index, a key fear gauge, jumping over 39% in a single day.

items.headline: NVIDIA Shares Plummet Amid Tech Sell-off
items.summary: Shares of NVIDIA fell significantly as the semiconductor sector experienced a broad downturn and profit booking after a strong rally.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Experiences Decline
items.summary: Meta shares dropped following a broader tech sell-off and reports suggesting a potential new stock offering to fund AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Tesla Stock Falls with Broader Tech Market
items.summary: Tesla shares were down as part of the wider retreat in technology stocks on Friday.

items.headline: Amazon Shares Decrease
items.summary: Amazon experienced a decline in its stock price amid the widespread sell-off in major technology companies.

items.headline: Microsoft Stock Records Losses
items.summary: Microsoft shares fell as part of the broader market's negative reaction to economic data and tech sector profit-taking.

items.headline: Alphabet Shares See Slight Dip
items.summary: Alphabet (Google's parent company) experienced a modest dip in its stock, outperforming many other tech giants during the sell-off.

items.headline: Strong May Jobs Report Fuels Rate Hike Speculation
items.summary: The U.S. economy added a surprising 172,000 jobs in May, significantly exceeding analyst expectations, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3% for the third consecutive month. This robust labor market data heightened expectations that the Federal Reserve might implement further interest rate hikes later in the year, leading to a surge in US Treasury yields and contributing to market anxiety. Markets are now fully pricing in a rate hike before year-end.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductor Sectors Lead Market Decline
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly AI and semiconductor stocks, bore the brunt of Friday's sell-off. The PHLX chip index plummeted 10.3%, marking its steepest one-day loss since March 2020. Muted guidance from Broadcom earlier in the week, combined with overbought sentiment and profit-booking, triggered a rout that erased approximately $1.3 trillion in market value from US-traded chipmakers. This led to a notable rotation out of AI-related growth stocks and towards more cyclical and defensive sectors.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Drive Individual Stock Movements
items.summary: Broadcom's ($AVGO) weak report and muted guidance earlier in the week significantly impacted the semiconductor sector. Additionally, Lululemon slumped 8.6% after trimming its revenue and profit forecasts, while DocuSign also saw its shares fall by 4% after a less-than-impressive outlook. Oracle and Adobe are among the major companies scheduled to release earnings in the coming week.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Continue to Weigh on Market Sentiment
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical uncertainties, particularly tensions in West Asia and developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict, continue to influence energy markets, inflation expectations, and overall investor sentiment. Elevated oil prices could exert upward pressure on inflation if supply disruptions persist, adding another layer of complexity for central bank policies.

items.headline: Anticipation Builds for SpaceX IPO Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is preparing for a blockbuster initial public offering (IPO) as early as next week, with an expected initial market capitalization of $1.77 trillion. Analysts suggest that the immense demand for SpaceX stock could necessitate institutional investors selling other holdings, potentially leading to further market disruption, especially for large tech companies.
